steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student's notes describe two distinct lever types with different structural positions. The question asks for a sentence that emphasizes how they contrast — so the correct answer must state the specific positional difference for each type.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — In levers, the effort is the force applied to the lever; the load, in contrast, is the force that the lever exerts on another object.: focuses on what both lever types have in common rather than how they differ. ✗
  • Choice B — In first-class and second-class levers, the fulcrum and the load are in different locations.: mentions a difference but does not specify the actual positions of the fulcrum or load. ✗
  • Choice C — First-class levers are simple machines consisting of a rigid beam and a fulcrum, but then again, the same is true of second-class lever.: frames the two types as alike rather than contrasting them. ✗
  • Choice D — In first-class levers, the fulcrum is located between the effort and the load, but in second-class levers, the load is located between the effort and the fulcrum.: directly states that first-class levers place the fulcrum between effort and load, while second-class levers place the load between effort and fulcrum — the exact positional contrast the notes show. ✓
That is the correct answer.
